docs.title: AGHub Documentation
docs.text:
New to AGHub? Follow these pages in order:

  1. Getting Started: account setup, first login, and cluster initialization.
  2. Research Drive: move data between your computer and AGHub.
  3. Installing Software: add packages with Conda or containers.

docs.title: Fast path
docs.text:
If you are setting up AGHub for the first time, most users can finish with this order:

  1. Complete all steps in Getting Started.
  2. Configure Research Drive and test one file transfer.
  3. Install extra software only if needed.

docs.title: AGHub Getting Started
docs.text: This guide helps you create access and log in to AGHub for the first time.

docs.title: Before you begin
docs.text:
You should have:

  • an invitation email for the AGHub SRAM collaboration
  • two AGHub account emails (username + password setup link)
  • a TOTP authenticator app (for example privacyIDEA)
  • an SSH key pair on your computer (or time to create one)

docs.title: Step 1: Join the SRAM collaboration
docs.text:
1. Open the invitation email and accept the collaboration invite.
  2. Click login and choose one identity provider:
  3. Preferred: your institutional account
  4. Fallback: EduID
  5. Complete 2FA during SRAM login.

Note: If your institute is not enabled for SRAM, contact your local ICT/helpdesk and ask them to enable SRAM/IdP access.

After a successful join, you receive AGHub onboarding emails.

Important: The password setup link is valid for 12 hours. AGHub synchronization can take about 20 minutes.

docs.title: Step 2: Set your AGHub password and 2FA
docs.text:
1. Use the one-time password setup link from email.
  2. Set a strong password (minimum 12 characters, with uppercase, lowercase, number, and symbol).
  3. Log in and register your 2FA app by scanning the QR code.
  4. Accept the SURF usage agreement.

Important: Do not close the page before finishing the one-time password setup flow.

docs.title: Step 3: Upload your SSH public key in SURF CUA
docs.text:
Open the SURF CUA portal and upload your public key.

If you do not have an SSH key yet:

  • Linux/macOS: follow Spider SSH key guide
  • Windows: use PuTTYgen

Show your public key on Linux/macOS:

cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub

Paste that value into the portal SSH key field and confirm with your portal password.

Important: Use the portal account password here, not any passphrase of your private SSH key.

docs.title: Step 4: First login to AGHub
docs.text:
Connect from your computer:

ssh sram-aghub-[first-initial][last-name]@doornode.hpcv.surf.nl

Then:

  1. Select aghub from the environment list.
  2. Enter your portal password.
  3. Enter the current 2FA code.

If successful, you will see the AGHub banner.

docs.title: Step 5: Initialize your AGHub account
docs.text:
Run:

/project/aghub/Share/init/init.sh

This script sets up:

  • a default Conda environment
  • ~/rd for Research Drive mounting
  • useful scripts in your home bin
  • default shell/editor/screen config files

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
1. Configure data transfer: Use of Research Drive
  2. Install extra tools if needed: Installation of Software Packages
  3. Submit your first jobs: Spider SLURM Getting Started

docs.title: Installation of Software Packages
docs.text:
AGHub has no direct internet connection. The usual workflow is:

  1. Prepare software on your local machine.
  2. Transfer it through Research Drive.
  3. Install/run it on AGHub.

If you have not set up transfer yet, first read Use of Research Drive.

docs.title: Recommended: install with conda-pack
docs.text: Use this when you need extra Python/R tools beyond the default AGHub environment.

docs.title: On your local machine
docs.text:
1. Create and activate a Conda environment:
conda create -n myenv
conda activate myenv
  1. Install the packages you need:
conda install numpy
  1. Install conda-pack and create an archive:
conda install conda-pack
conda-pack -n myenv -o myenv.txz
  1. Copy the archive into your Research Drive folder.

Note: If Conda is not installed locally, use Miniforge.

docs.title: On AGHub
docs.text:
1. Create target directory and unpack:
mkdir -p ~/envs/myenv
tar -xvf ~/rd/myenv.txz -C ~/envs/myenv
  1. Activate and finalize:
conda activate ~/envs/myenv
conda-unpack

docs.title: Alternative: run software with Singularity/Apptainer
docs.text:
AGHub provides a prebuilt image at /project/aghub/Share/images/conda.sif.

Open a shell in the container:

singularity shell /project/aghub/Share/images/conda.sif

Run a command directly:

singularity run /project/aghub/Share/images/conda.sif ipython

Important: You cannot run SLURM submission commands (for example sbatch) from inside the container.

docs.title: Continue with SLURM
docs.text: After software setup, submit jobs with the Spider guide: Spider SLURM Getting Started

docs.title: Use of Research Drive
docs.text: AGHub has no direct internet access. Use SURF Research Drive to move files between your computer and AGHub.

docs.title: What you need
docs.text:
• Research Drive invitation email
  • AGHub account already working
  • rclone configured on your computer and on AGHub (recommended)

docs.title: Step 1: Activate your Research Drive account
docs.text:
1. Open your invitation email and create the account.
  2. Sign in with your institutional account if possible, otherwise set a password.
  3. Wait until AGHub admins grant access to your personal AGHub folder (can take up to one business day).
  4. Verify access on the web portal: Research Drive login

Note: In password setup flow, the button may appear inactive; in that case you receive a temporary password by email.

docs.title: Step 2: Choose how to access Research Drive locally
docs.text:
Options:

  • Web browser: Research Drive
  • Desktop sync client: ownCloud desktop client
  • Recommended: rclone mount/copy: Access Research Drive via rclone

docs.title: Step 3: Configure rclone on AGHub
docs.text:
Follow the same SURF rclone instructions on AGHub and create a remote name (examples below use RD).

Test:

rclone ls RD:

docs.title: Option A (simple): direct copy
docs.text:
rclone copy /path/to/local/file RD:your_folder_name/file
rclone ls RD:your_folder_name

docs.title: Option B (recommended): mount Research Drive on AGHub
docs.text:
mount_rd RD:your_folder_name

This mounts Research Drive in ~/rd and keeps it running in the background.

Unmount:

unmount_rd

Note: mount_rd assumes remote name RD. If yours is different, replace it. Note: ~/rd is created by the AGHub init script in Getting Started. Warning: Mounted Research Drive is visible on the UI/login machine only, not on worker nodes. For jobs, use rclone copy.

docs.title: Manual mount (optional)
docs.text: rclone mount --use-cookies --timeout 15m --cache-dir ~/.rd_cache --vfs-cache-mode full --no-modtime RD:your_folder_name ~/rd

docs.title: Next step
docs.text: After file transfer works, continue with Installation of Software Packages.
